content: I will write a Node.js script to programmatically add the `downloadUrl` property to the `Game` interface and all 21 games in the `games.ts` file. This avoids manual text replacement errors and sets up placeholder download links like `https://example.com/download-box1` through `https://example.com/download-box21` for the user to easily customize.
thinking:
**Counting Game Entries**

I've determined there are precisely 21 games listed in `games.ts`. Now, I'm considering the implications of adding `downloadUrl` to the `Game` interface. Adding this field requires updating each of the existing 21 game entries, to keep the types happy. I'm starting the list and preparing to populate the data.


**Planning Download URLs**

I've decided to add dummy download URLs, like `https://example.com/download-meccha-chameleon`, for the games. I'll modify the `Game` interface in `games.ts` by adding `downloadUrl: string`. To update the file safely, I'll write a Node script that reads `games.ts`, adds the new field to each game, and writes the updated content back. This avoids manual edits. I've confirmed that Node.js is available.

args.ArtifactMetadata: {"ArtifactType":"other","RequestFeedback":false,"Summary":"Scratch script to add downloadUrl to games.ts."}
args.Description: "Create Node script to update games.ts with downloadUrl fields."
